content.problemStatement:
Debug the code in the IDE.

The code is supposed to do the following:


Input three space-separated integers on a single line.
If the middle integer is greater than the sum of the other two integers and is odd, print 1; otherwise, print 0.
content.inputFormat: A single line containing three space-separated integers a, b and c.
content.outputFormat: Print 1 if b is greater than a + c and b is odd; otherwise print 0.

hidden.label: odd and greater with small numbers

solution.approach: Read the three integers a, b and c. Check the two conditions: b > a + c and b is odd (b % 2 != 0). If both hold, output 1; otherwise output 0. This is O(1) time and O(1) extra space.

code.Python3:
import sys
data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
if len(data) < 3:
    sys.exit()
a, b, c = map(int, data[:3])
ans = 1 if (b > a + c and b % 2 != 0) else 0
print(ans)
